question: A Mendelian experiment consisted of breeding pea plants bearing violet flowers with pea plants bearing white flowers. What will be the result in F1 progeny?
subject: science
answer: All plants in F1 progeny will bear violet flowers. Violet flower colour is dominant over white flower colour.

question: Write the energy conversion that takes place in a hydropower plant.
subject: science
answer: Potential energy of water → Kinetic energy of water → Electrical energy.

question: A compound 'X' on heating with excess concentrated sulphuric acid at 443 K gives an unsaturated compound 'Y'. 'X' also reacts with sodium metal to evolve a colourless gas 'Z'. Identify 'X', 'Y' and 'Z'. Write the equation of the chemical reaction of the formation of 'Y' and also write the role of sulphuric acid in the reaction.
subject: science
answer: X = Ethanol (C₂H₅OH), Y = Ethene (CH₂=CH₂), Z = Hydrogen gas. Reaction: C₂H₅OH → CH₂=CH₂ + H₂O (conc. H₂SO₄, 443K). H₂SO₄ acts as a dehydrating agent.

question: Write one equation each for decomposition reactions where energy is supplied in the form of heat, light and electricity.
subject: science
answer: Heat (Thermolysis): CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂↑. Light (Photolysis): 2AgCl → 2Ag + Cl₂↑ (sunlight). Electricity (Electrolysis): 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂↑ (electric current).

question: 2 ml of sodium hydroxide solution is added to a few pieces of granulated zinc metal taken in a test tube. When the contents are warmed a gas evolves which is bubbled through a soap solution before testing. Write the equation of the chemical reaction involved and the test to detect the gas. Name the gas which will be evolved when the same metal reacts with a dilute solution of a strong acid.
subject: science
topic: Reactivity of metals with alkalis and acids
answer: Zn + 2NaOH → Na₂ZnO₂ + H₂↑. Test: Apply a burning splinter to the gas — it burns with a pop sound confirming Hydrogen gas. With dilute strong acid also Hydrogen gas is evolved.

question: Name the hormones secreted by the following endocrine glands and specify one function of each: (1) Thyroid (2) Pituitary (3) Pancreas
subject: science
answer: (1) Thyroid → Thyroxine: regulates carbohydrate, fat and protein metabolism. (2) Pituitary → Growth Hormone (GH): controls growth and development. (3) Pancreas → Insulin: regulates blood glucose levels.

question: Write one main difference between the asexual and sexual mode of reproduction. Which species is likely to have comparatively better chances of survival — the one reproducing asexually or the one reproducing sexually? Give reasons to justify your answer.
subject: science
answer: Difference: Asexual involves one parent, produces genetically identical offspring; sexual involves two parents, produces genetically varied offspring. Species reproducing sexually has better survival chances because genetic variation provides diversity enabling some individuals to adapt to changing environments.

question: List the factors on which the resistance of a conductor in the shape of a wire depends. Why are metals good conductors of electricity whereas glass is a bad conductor of electricity? Why are alloys commonly used in electrical heating devices?
subject: science
answer: Factors: length (R ∝ L), cross-sectional area (R ∝ 1/A), nature of material (resistivity), temperature. Metals have free electrons that carry charge; glass has no free electrons. Alloys have high resistivity and do not oxidise easily at high temperatures, making them ideal for heating elements.

question: Show how would you join three resistors, each of resistance 9 Ω so that the equivalent resistance of the combination is (a) 13.5 Ω (b) 6 Ω.
subject: science
topic: Series and parallel combination of resistors
answer: (a) 13.5 Ω: Connect two 9Ω resistors in parallel (gives 4.5Ω) then in series with the third 9Ω → 4.5 + 9 = 13.5 Ω. (b) 6 Ω: Connect two 9Ω resistors in series (gives 18Ω) then in parallel with the third 9Ω → (18×9)/(18+9) = 6 Ω.

question: State Fleming's left-hand rule. Write the principle of working of an electric motor. Explain the function of the following parts of an electric motor: (i) Armature (ii) Brushes (iii) Split ring.
subject: science
answer: Fleming's left-hand rule: Stretch forefinger, middle finger and thumb of left hand mutually perpendicular. Forefinger→field direction, middle finger→current direction, thumb→force direction. Principle: A current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field experiences a force. (i) Armature: rectangular coil that rotates and converts electrical energy to mechanical energy. (ii) Brushes: carbon brushes maintain electrical contact with the rotating split ring. (iii) Split ring (commutator): reverses current direction every half rotation to keep the coil rotating in the same direction.

question: The modern periodic table has been evolved through the early attempts of Dobereiner, Newland and Mendeleev. List one advantage and one limitation of all three attempts. Name the scientist who first showed that atomic number of an element is a more fundamental property than its atomic mass. State Modern Periodic law.
subject: science
answer: Dobereiner's Triads: Advantage—middle element's atomic mass ≈ average of other two; Limitation—could not classify all known elements. Newlands' Octaves: Advantage—first to correlate properties with atomic mass periodically; Limitation—failed after calcium, noble gases not accommodated. Mendeleev's Table: Advantage—predicted undiscovered elements, left gaps; Limitation—could not place hydrogen, isotopes, wrong order of some elements. Scientist: Henry Moseley. Modern Periodic Law: Properties of elements are periodic functions of their atomic numbers.

question: Write the steps involved in the extraction of pure metals in the middle of the activity series from carbonate ores. How is copper extracted from its sulfide ore? Explain the various steps supported by chemical equations. Draw a labelled diagram for electrolytic refining of copper.
subject: science
answer: Carbonate ore extraction: (i) Calcination: heat ore in absence of air → metal oxide + CO₂. (ii) Reduction with carbon: metal oxide + C → metal + CO₂. Copper from sulfide: (i) Roasting: 2Cu₂S + 3O₂ → 2Cu₂O + 2SO₂. (ii) Reduction: 2Cu₂O + Cu₂S → 6Cu + SO₂. (iii) Electrolytic refining: impure copper as anode, pure copper as cathode, CuSO₄ solution as electrolyte; pure Cu deposits on cathode.

question: A student is unable to see clearly the words written on the blackboard placed at a distance of approximately 3 m from him. Name the defect of vision the boy is suffering from. State the possible causes of this defect and explain the method of correcting it. Why do stars twinkle? Explain.
subject: science
answer: Defect: Myopia (short-sightedness). Causes: (i) elongation of eyeball, (ii) excessive curvature of lens. Correction: Concave lens of appropriate focal length. Stars twinkle: Starlight undergoes atmospheric refraction through layers of varying refractive index. The continuously changing atmosphere bends starlight differently, causing the apparent position and brightness to fluctuate, producing the twinkling effect.

question: Define excretion. Name the basic filtration unit present in the kidney. Draw a labelled diagram of the excretory system of the human body.
subject: science
answer: Excretion: The biological process by which metabolic waste products and excess substances are removed from the body. Basic filtration unit: Nephron. The excretory system consists of two kidneys, two ureters, urinary bladder and urethra. Each kidney contains about one million nephrons where filtration, reabsorption and secretion occur to form urine.

question: A student added few pieces of aluminium metal to two test tubes A and B containing aqueous solutions of iron sulphate and copper sulphate respectively. In the second part of the experiment, she added iron metal to test tubes C and D containing aqueous solutions of aluminium sulphate and copper sulphate. In which test tube(s) will she observe a colour change? State which is the most reactive metal and why.
subject: science
answer: Colour change observed in test tubes A, B, and D. No change in C (Al cannot displace Al from its own salt). Most reactive metal: Aluminium — it displaces both Fe and Cu from their salt solutions, showing Al > Fe > Cu in reactivity.

question: An object of height 4.0 cm is placed at a distance of 30 cm from the optical centre 'O' of a convex lens of focal length 20 cm. Draw a ray diagram to find the position and size of the image formed. Mark optical centre 'O' and principal focus 'F' on the diagram. Also find the approximate ratio of size of image to the size of object.
subject: science
answer: Using lens formula: 1/v − 1/u = 1/f; u = −30 cm, f = +20 cm → 1/v = 1/20 − 1/30 = 1/60; v = +60 cm. Magnification m = v/u = 60/(−30) = −2. Image is real, inverted and 2× magnified on the other side. Ratio of image size to object size = 2:1.

question: What is the function of a galvanometer in a circuit?
subject: science
answer: A galvanometer is used to detect and measure the presence of electric current in a circuit. It can also indicate the direction of current flow.

question: Why is biogas considered an excellent fuel?
subject: science
answer: Biogas is considered an excellent fuel because: (i) It produces no smoke and causes no pollution. (ii) It has a high calorific value. (iii) It is produced from bio-waste, aiding waste management. (iv) The slurry left after biogas production is a good manure.

question: Write two different ways in which glucose is oxidized to provide energy in the human body.
subject: science
answer: (i) Aerobic respiration (with oxygen): C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + Energy (ATP). Occurs in mitochondria. (ii) Anaerobic respiration (without oxygen): C₆H₁₂O₆ → 2C₃H₆O₃ (lactic acid) + Energy (in muscle cells) or → 2C₂H₅OH + 2CO₂ + Energy (in yeast).

question: Define the term 'power of accommodation'. Write the modification in the curvature of the eye lens when we observe near objects and then shift our gaze to distant objects.
subject: science
answer: Power of accommodation: The ability of the eye to adjust its focal length to see clearly both near and distant objects by changing the curvature of the lens using ciliary muscles. Near objects: Ciliary muscles contract, lens becomes thicker (more curved), focal length decreases. Distant objects: Ciliary muscles relax, lens becomes thinner (less curved), focal length increases.

question: Write three types of blood vessels present in the human body. Give one important feature of each type.
subject: science
answer: (i) Arteries: Carry oxygenated blood away from the heart; have thick, elastic walls to withstand high pressure. (ii) Veins: Carry deoxygenated blood towards the heart; have thin walls and valves to prevent backflow. (iii) Capillaries: Finest blood vessels forming network in tissues; walls are one cell thick, enabling exchange of materials between blood and cells.

question: Trace the sequence of events which occur when a bright light is focused on one of your eyes. What will happen if you close your eyes, will you observe the same change? Justify.
subject: science
answer: Sequence: Bright light → Photoreceptors in retina stimulated → Nerve impulse via optic nerve → Brain interprets → Efferent nerve sends signal to iris muscles → Pupil constricts (reduces light entry). If eyes are closed: No, same pupil constriction will not occur in the same way, as no light reaches the retina and the reflex arc is not triggered.

question: What are plant hormones? Name the plant hormones responsible for: (i) growth of stem (ii) promotion of cell division (iii) inhibition of growth (iv) elongation of cells.
subject: science
answer: Plant hormones: Chemical substances produced in plants that coordinate and regulate growth and developmental responses. (i) Growth of stem: Gibberellins. (ii) Promotion of cell division: Cytokinins. (iii) Inhibition of growth: Abscisic acid. (iv) Elongation of cells: Auxins.

question: How can it be proved that the basic structure of the Modern Periodic Table is based on the electronic configuration of atoms of different elements?
subject: science
answer: In the Modern Periodic Table, elements are arranged in order of increasing atomic number. Elements in the same period have the same number of shells (valence shell), and elements in the same group have the same number of valence electrons, giving them similar chemical properties. This arrangement directly reflects the electronic configuration of elements, proving the periodic table is based on electronic configuration.

question: What is a rainbow? Draw a labelled diagram to show the formation of a rainbow. When can a rainbow be observed?
subject: science
answer: A rainbow is a natural spectrum of colours formed in the sky when sunlight undergoes dispersion by water droplets suspended in the atmosphere after rain. White sunlight enters water droplets, undergoes refraction, total internal reflection, and refraction again, separating into VIBGYOR. It can be observed after rainfall when the sun is behind the observer and rain falls in front.

question: What is a solenoid? Draw the pattern of magnetic field lines around a solenoid. Compare the magnetic field of a solenoid with that of a bar magnet. List two uses of electromagnets.
subject: science
answer: A solenoid is a coil of many circular turns of insulated copper wire wound in the shape of a cylinder. The magnetic field of a solenoid is similar to that of a bar magnet — it has a north pole at one end and south pole at the other, with parallel field lines inside. Difference: solenoid's strength is adjustable (by changing current), unlike a permanent bar magnet. Uses: Electric bell, electric cranes for lifting iron/scrap.

question: Identify the acid and base from which sodium chloride is obtained. Is the solution of sodium chloride neutral, acidic or basic? In which form is sodium chloride found in nature? Write chemical equation for the reaction involved.
subject: science
answer: Acid: Hydrochloric acid (HCl). Base: Sodium hydroxide (NaOH). Reaction: HCl + NaOH → NaCl + H₂O. The solution of NaCl is neutral (pH = 7). In nature, sodium chloride is found as rock salt (halite) — large crystals found in ancient dried sea beds and as sea salt dissolved in seawater.

question: A student uses a concave lens of focal length 40 cm. Another student uses a convex lens of focal length 20 cm. An object is placed at a distance of 60 cm from the concave lens. Find the image distance, and write two characteristics of the image formed.
subject: science
answer: For concave lens: u = −60 cm, f = −40 cm (negative for concave lens). Using lens formula: 1/v − 1/u = 1/f → 1/v = 1/f + 1/u = (−1/40) + (−1/60) = −3/120 − 2/120 = −5/120; v = −24 cm. The image is formed at 24 cm on the same side as the object (negative sign confirms virtual image). Characteristics: (i) Virtual and erect. (ii) Diminished (smaller than object).

question: Show how would you connect three resistors of resistances 3 Ω, 6 Ω, and 12 Ω so that the combination: (a) has equivalent resistance of 2 Ω, (b) draws maximum current from a 12 V battery. Calculate the current drawn in each case.
subject: science
answer: (a) For equivalent resistance of 2 Ω: Connect the 3 Ω and 6 Ω resistors in parallel. 1/R = 1/3 + 1/6 = 2/6 + 1/6 = 3/6; R = 2 Ω. The 12 Ω resistor is not connected (open circuit). Current drawn from 12 V: I = V/R = 12/2 = 6 A. (Note: It is not possible to obtain exactly 2 Ω using all three resistors simultaneously.) (b) Maximum current is drawn when total resistance is minimum, i.e., all three resistors in parallel. 1/R = 1/3 + 1/6 + 1/12 = 4/12 + 2/12 + 1/12 = 7/12; R = 12/7 Ω ≈ 1.71 Ω. I = V/R = 12 ÷ (12/7) = 7 A.

question: Explain the process of pollination in plants. Distinguish between self-pollination and cross-pollination. What role do flowers play in reproduction? Name two agents of pollination.
subject: science
answer: Pollination: Transfer of pollen grains from the anther of a stamen to the stigma of a pistil. Self-pollination: pollen transfers to stigma of the same flower or another flower on the same plant. Cross-pollination: pollen transfers to stigma of a flower on a different plant of the same species — leads to genetic variation. Flowers are the reproductive organs of angiosperms; they produce pollen (male gametes) and ovules (female gametes). Agents: Wind (anemophily) and insects (entomophily).

question: Write two methods of waste disposal. As an individual, what measures can you take to generate the least garbage? List two values instilled by minimising waste.
subject: science
answer: Methods: (i) Composting of biodegradable waste. (ii) Recycling of paper, glass, plastics and metals. Individual measures: (i) Use cloth/jute bags instead of plastic bags. (ii) Avoid over-packaged products and prefer reusable containers. Values: (i) Environmental responsibility. (ii) Sustainable living and conservation of natural resources.

question: What is water harvesting? List two main advantages of water harvesting. Why has it not been more widely adopted despite its benefits?
subject: science
answer: Water harvesting: The process of collecting and storing rainwater or surface runoff for future use. Advantages: (i) Recharges groundwater levels, increasing water availability. (ii) Prevents flooding and soil erosion, increases agricultural productivity. Not widely adopted due to: Lack of awareness, high initial infrastructure cost, and loss of traditional knowledge about water management systems.

question: Name a cyclic unsaturated carbon compound.
subject: science
answer: Benzene (C₆H₆) — a cyclic unsaturated hydrocarbon with alternating single and double bonds in a ring.

question: List two sources of magnetic fields.
subject: science
answer: (i) Bar magnet (permanent magnet). (ii) Electromagnet (current-carrying coil/solenoid).

question: Baking soda is a mixture of which substances? Write the chemical formula of baking soda.
subject: science
answer: Baking soda is sodium hydrogen carbonate. Its chemical formula is NaHCO₃. (Note: Baking powder is a different product — a mixture of NaHCO₃ and tartaric acid.)

question: Copper powder is heated in a china dish. The reddish-brown surface of copper turns black. What is the black substance formed? When hydrogen gas is passed over this black substance, a reddish-brown substance is formed. Write balanced chemical equations for both reactions.
subject: science
answer: Black substance: Copper(II) oxide (CuO). Oxidation: 2Cu + O₂ → 2CuO. Reduction with hydrogen: CuO + H₂ → Cu + H₂O. This pair of reactions illustrates redox — Cu is oxidised to CuO, then CuO is reduced back to Cu.

question: Define the Tyndall effect. Give any four instances where you can see this phenomenon.
subject: science
answer: Tyndall effect: The scattering of light by colloidal particles suspended in a medium, making the path of light visible. Instances: (i) Beam of sunlight through a canopy of trees in a forest. (ii) Headlight beam of a car on a foggy night. (iii) A beam of sunlight entering a dusty room through a small opening. (iv) Blue colour of smoke from a chimney observed against a dark background.

question: What is hypermetropia? State its causes. Explain using a ray diagram how this defect can be corrected using a lens.
subject: science
answer: Hypermetropia (long-sightedness): A defect where a person can see distant objects clearly but cannot see nearby objects distinctly. Causes: (i) The eyeball becomes too short. (ii) The eye lens loses flexibility and cannot curve enough (ciliary muscles too weak). Correction: A convex (converging) lens of suitable focal length is used. It converges the light rays so they form a focused image on the retina.

question: What is adrenaline? Under what condition is it secreted? State the effect of adrenaline secretion on the human body.
subject: science
answer: Adrenaline is a hormone secreted by the adrenal glands (located on top of kidneys). It is secreted in response to stress, fear, excitement or emergency situations (fight-or-flight response). Effects: (i) Heart rate increases to pump more blood to muscles. (ii) Breathing rate increases. (iii) Blood is diverted away from gut to muscles and brain. (iv) Pupils dilate for better vision. The body is prepared for rapid physical action.

question: What is the role of chromosomes in determining the sex of a newborn? Explain with the help of a flow chart.
subject: science
answer: In humans, sex is determined by sex chromosomes. Females have two X chromosomes (XX) and males have one X and one Y chromosome (XY). All eggs produced by females contain the X chromosome. Sperm can be either X-bearing or Y-bearing. If an X-sperm fertilises the egg → XX → female child. If a Y-sperm fertilises the egg → XY → male child. Therefore, the sex of the child is determined by the father's sperm, not the mother.

question: Write Joule's law of heating. Two lamps of ratings 60 W, 220 V and 40 W, 220 V are connected: (i) in series, (ii) in parallel to 220 V supply. Find the total power consumed in each case.
subject: science
topic: Joule's law and power in circuits
answer: Joule's law: H = I²Rt — the heat produced in a resistor is directly proportional to the square of current, resistance and time. R₁ = V²/P₁ = 220²/60 ≈ 806.7 Ω; R₂ = 220²/40 = 1210 Ω. (i) Series: R_total = 2016.7 Ω; P = V²/R = 220²/2016.7 ≈ 24 W. (ii) Parallel: P_total = P₁ + P₂ = 60 + 40 = 100 W.

question: Define an electromagnet. Draw a labelled diagram to show how an electromagnet is constructed. State two uses of electromagnets. What is the purpose of using a soft iron core instead of a steel core in an electromagnet?
subject: science
answer: An electromagnet is a temporary magnet made by winding insulated copper wire around a soft iron core and passing electric current through it. Construction: soft iron core, insulated copper wire coil wound around it, connected to battery and switch. Uses: (i) Electric cranes for lifting heavy iron objects in scrap yards. (ii) Electric bells, MRI machines in hospitals. Soft iron core: Soft iron readily becomes magnetised when current flows and loses magnetism immediately when current stops — useful for temporary magnets. Steel retains magnetism (not suitable for temporary use).

question: Why can carbon not reduce the oxides of sodium, magnesium and aluminium? How are these metals extracted from their ores? Explain the process with a chemical equation.
subject: science
answer: Carbon cannot reduce oxides of Na, Mg, Al because these metals are more reactive (higher in activity series) than carbon. They hold oxygen more strongly than carbon does. These metals are extracted by electrolysis of their molten salts. Example — sodium: 2NaCl(l) → 2Na + Cl₂ (electrolysis). Na is deposited at cathode; Cl₂ is released at anode. Similarly, aluminium is extracted by electrolysis of molten Al₂O₃ (bauxite).

question: Explain with balanced chemical equations: (i) formation of washing soda from baking soda, (ii) how washing soda removes permanent hardness of water.
subject: science
answer: (i) Baking soda → Washing soda: 2NaHCO₃ → Na₂CO₃ + H₂O + CO₂ (on heating); then Na₂CO₃ + 10H₂O → Na₂CO₃·10H₂O (washing soda). (ii) Washing soda reacts with calcium/magnesium salts (permanent hardness): Na₂CO₃ + CaCl₂ → CaCO₃↓ + 2NaCl. The insoluble CaCO₃ precipitates out, removing Ca²⁺ ions and softening the water.

question: State Mendel's law of dominance and law of segregation. Explain both laws with the example of a tall pea plant crossed with a dwarf pea plant. Draw a flow chart to illustrate the law of segregation.
subject: science
answer: Law of Dominance: When two homozygous individuals with contrasting traits are crossed, only the dominant trait appears in the F1 generation. The recessive trait is suppressed. Law of Segregation: The two alleles of a gene separate during gamete formation; each gamete receives only one allele. Example: Tall (TT) × Dwarf (tt) → F1: all Tt (Tall). F1 × F1: Tt × Tt → F2: TT, Tt, Tt, tt → 3 Tall : 1 Dwarf. The recessive trait (dwarf) reappears in F2, demonstrating segregation of alleles.

question: What is rancidity? State two ways to prevent rancidity of food. Food materials containing fats and oils are left for long periods — what observable changes occur?
subject: science
answer: Rancidity: The oxidation of fats and oils in food when exposed to air, causing unpleasant smell and taste. Prevention: (i) Adding antioxidants (e.g., BHA, BHT) to food. (ii) Storing food in airtight containers or flushing packets with nitrogen gas (e.g., chips packets). Observable changes: (i) Food develops an unpleasant, stale smell. (ii) Taste becomes bitter and off. (iii) Yellow discolouration may occur in some foods.

question: Why do aquatic animals breathe faster than terrestrial animals? Draw a well-labelled diagram of the respiratory system of humans.
subject: science
answer: Aquatic animals breathe faster because water has less dissolved oxygen compared to air. To extract sufficient oxygen, fish must pass larger volumes of water over gills rapidly. In contrast, air has about 21% oxygen, so terrestrial animals need to breathe less frequently. Human respiratory system: nasal cavity → pharynx → larynx → trachea → bronchi → bronchioles → alveoli (in lungs). Diaphragm and intercostal muscles control breathing. Gaseous exchange occurs at alveoli.

question: Draw ray diagrams to show the nature, position and size of the image formed by a concave mirror for the following positions of the object: (i) Object between focus F and the pole P of the mirror. (ii) Object at centre of curvature C.
subject: science
answer: (i) Object between F and P: Image is virtual, erect, magnified, and formed behind the mirror. This is the basis of use as a shaving/makeup mirror. (ii) Object at C: Image forms at C itself, is real, inverted, and same size as the object (magnification = −1). Mirror formula: 1/v + 1/u = 1/f. For object at C: u = −2f → v = −2f → image at C.

question: Explain the process of electrolysis of water. Name the gases liberated at (i) anode and (ii) cathode. Why is the volume of gas collected at one electrode double that at the other electrode?
subject: science
answer: Electrolysis of water: 2H₂O(l) → 2H₂(g) + O₂(g) (using electric current; dilute H₂SO₄ added to improve conductivity). (i) Anode (+): Oxygen gas (O₂). (ii) Cathode (−): Hydrogen gas (H₂). Volume ratio: From the equation, 2 moles H₂ are produced for every 1 mole O₂. Since equal moles of gas occupy equal volumes at same conditions, hydrogen volume is double that of oxygen.

question: Define the term 'excretion'. Name the organs involved in the human excretory system. Explain the mechanism by which kidneys produce urine.
subject: science
answer: Excretion: The biological process by which metabolic waste products are eliminated from the body. Organs: Two kidneys, two ureters, urinary bladder, urethra. Urine production: (i) Filtration — blood entering kidney glomerulus is filtered under high pressure; small molecules (glucose, urea, salts, water) pass into Bowman's capsule. (ii) Selective reabsorption — useful substances (glucose, amino acids, water) are reabsorbed in tubules. (iii) Tubular secretion — additional waste is secreted into filtrate. Final product is urine (mainly water, urea, salts), which drains to urinary bladder via ureters.
